title: Hybrid Retrieval for RAG
text: Hybrid retrieval combines sparse keyword matching such as BM25 with dense vector retrieval. In RAG systems, hybrid search improves recall because exact technical terms and semantic paraphrases are handled by different retrievers.

title: Reranking in Retrieval Pipelines
text: A reranker scores candidate passages after initial retrieval. Cross-encoder rerankers are accurate but expensive. Lightweight lexical rerankers can still improve ordering by checking query term coverage and phrase overlap.

title: Query Rewrite
text: Query rewrite expands or reformulates a user question before retrieval. It can add synonyms, clarify abbreviations, and generate multiple search routes for complex questions.

title: Agent State Graphs
text: Agent workflows can be represented as state graphs. Planner, tool executor, verifier, and summarizer nodes communicate through explicit state, which improves observability and recovery.

title: CTR Prediction
text: CTR prediction estimates the probability that a user clicks an item. Common models include LightGBM, XGBoost, DeepFM, xDeepFM, DIN, and DCN. Offline metrics include AUC and LogLoss.

title: Time Series Anomaly Detection
text: Time series anomaly detection compares observed values with expected patterns. Forecasting error, seasonal decomposition, and threshold search are common components.

title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
text: FAISS is a library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. It is frequently used to scale vector search in retrieval augmented generation systems.

title: Retrieval Evaluation Metrics
text: Recall at K measures whether relevant documents are retrieved. MRR rewards the rank of the first relevant result. nDCG evaluates graded relevance and ranking quality.
